title: Sebastiania phyllanthiformis

This family includes over 300 genera and around 2,000 species. Sebastiania phyllanthiformis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Sebastiania, which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Euphorbiaceae family is commonly known as the spurge family due to the presence of a milky latex in many of its members. Sebastiania phyllanthiformis is an important species in its native habitats, providing food and shelter for various animals and insects.
